sourceMovie.title: The Fighter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Fighter works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Creed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Fighter clicked for you, Creed (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Fighter?
faq.answer: Try Creed, Southpaw, Rocky IV—each shares DNA with The Fighter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Fighter?
faq.answer: The Fighter (2010) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Fighter worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
